title: Burst Balloons
prompt:
You are given `n` balloons, indexed from `0` to `n - 1`. Each balloon is painted with a number on it represented by array `nums`. You are asked to burst all the balloons.

If you burst the `i`th balloon, you will get `nums[i - 1] * nums[i] * nums[i + 1]` coins. If `i - 1` or `i + 1` goes out of bounds of the array, then treat it as if there is a balloon with a `1` painted on it.

Return the maximum coins you can collect by bursting the balloons wisely.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Interval DP. Pad nums with 1 on both ends. dp[i][j] = max coins from bursting all balloons between index i and j (exclusive). For each subproblem, choose which balloon k to burst LAST in the range (i,j). dp[i][j] = max(dp[i][k] + dp[k][j] + nums[i]*nums[k]*nums[j]) for all k in (i,j). The key insight is thinking about the last balloon to burst, not the first.
commonBugs: Thinking about which balloon to burst first instead of last (the first-burst approach is much harder because neighbors change)
Forgetting to pad the array with 1s on both ends
Incorrect loop ordering—must iterate by increasing interval length
